text:
Market today: Chip sector is getting hit hard - Samsung and SK Hynix dropping 7%+ signals the semiconductor rout that started on Wall Street is spreading globally. That's the real driver today. Your tech-heavy, margin-amplified book is directly in the crosshairs.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is your most urgent concern - it's a 3X leveraged semiconductor ETF and it's bleeding; stop is at $200, price at $217.55, not much cushion on margin. SPAL (-13.72%) is brand-new, very volatile - essentially an IPO-stage leveraged ETF moving fast with a razor-thin stop at $21.38 vs current $21.70; one bad tick and you're out. SMH (-2.5%) is the unleveraged chip exposure adding quiet drag.

Watch: Whether SMH breaks toward $540 stop - that would confirm chip weakness is deepening, not bouncing. Also watch if SOXL loses $200; on margin that accelerates pain fast.

Bottom line: Chip sector is under real pressure today - protect the leveraged positions first, everything else can wait.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your line - consider tightening it or trimming now given the global chip rout news; margin amplifies any further drop significantly.
SPAL: Stop $21.38 is basically right here at $21.70 - either confirm you're comfortable being stopped out today or cut it manually; this moves too fast to ignore.
No new buys today - chip sector under pressure globally, adding longs into a rout on margin is the wrong move right now.
